item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Congrats! You deserve it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good, I must say. Simple, interesting and complete. The mysteries left do not give the impression that the work was left undone, at most ongoing. Weird and unexplained, but explored in enough detail.

Upvoted.
A little late, but yayy! My pagan beetles got +100! Thanks, y'all, for reading this thang.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was Nice and fun to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"members of the hylotrupes family"

Really should be "members of the Hylotrupes genus" instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pagan beetles! I like. It's simple, cute, and interesting. It reminds me of SCP-2023 but less mysterious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dark! I'm happy to see this up on the mainlist and doing well. Congrats my friend. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was hoping to make them cute and lovable, so yay, confirmation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are such nice, friendly bugs! Personally I find simple SCPs like this one refreshing. It's a quick characterization that still made me feel something. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one, which is odd, because I usually dislike newly created SCPs. It's short and to the point and leaves little to the imagination. The description is spot on and it does a good job conveying a sort of magic

+1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not judging this SCP just because it's short. In fact, I personally prefer shorter articles in general. The problem is this article does not present anything that would make me care about what is happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will at some point within the next 12 hours address this concern, and try and reword what I can find and figure out how to reword. I'll probably PM you when this is done, and if you still find some less than professional phrasing in there, could you please point each one out to me, line through sort of thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very close to an upvote from me - the overall image was quite good, and I didn't feel the need for greater explication or backstory. What undermines it is the writing, which is sometimes a touch inexpertly phrased.

For example:

It appears to be a shrine, with a roof held up by four pillars, which has a basin on a pedestal in the middle.

This scans oddly to me - seemingly trying to fit slightly too many ideas in the one sentence (perhaps because of the comma placement, or the use of "which has" instead of "and"). There are a few examples of this construction that read similarly oddly - the first sentence of the Description is another.

all died of heavy trauma or resulting blood loss

I'm not sure that "heavy trauma" is sufficiently precise, and you clarify later that in two cases it was head trauma. Generally the addendum shows signs of having been written "on the hoof", and could do with some tightening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have an idea, I guess I'll just write it up and see if it works…

EDIT: Wrote it. Tell me all of your thoughttssssss

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the fence about this. It's solidly written and visually distinct, but I do feel like it's missing a little something that would really tie it together that I can't quite put my finger on.

I'd err away from any blatant or outrageous, but perhaps something should be included after the list? It wasn't a particularly strong note to end on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A longer article is usually more challenging to present, since keeping a reader engaged throughout it is tough. When the author manages a great ending, it tends to get an overall better reception, for immersion and essentially telling a story.

Presenting a shorter article is riskier, because the story-telling is left to the reader once they're given the info. It could

  • An older example of a short article that manages to quickly engage a reader with an unusual idea. The last addendum seals up the background nicely.
  • An example of another animal that carved into wood, longer than the current one but with more context to portray it as tragic. Empathy is created, while there are many open-ended questions still remaining.

I think what makes this article less remarkable is, while it has some historical context, the anomaly itself is beetles with tribal behavior. Because most readers don't know much about the Celtics and France, they aren't left questioning why this is happening, when SCP-1468 is clearer about the weirdness of the situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heheh, I guess I am "Dark". Time to find Marshall and Carter, and we can be a writing team.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and interesting SCP. Good job on it Dark. +1

item.title: Re: Let's Hope This Lives
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, but say that to the +3 rating. Also, not sure if Tomato was talking about the length of it.

EDIT: But hello… the two highest karma people who have rated have +1ed. I am regaining hope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But why? Why should an SCP be judged on it's length and not it's value?

item.title: Re: Let's Hope This Lives
item.content:encoded: dude. I love it. I love all short SCPs. If anyone dislikes it because of it's length, it's because this website has, over time, conditioned the users to expect longer and longer SCPs. it was an interesting read that got to the point quickly, established a theme, and actually made me feel something. congratulations, I loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, there's not enough here for me to form any kind of lasting impression. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think this has a lot of substance outside of being cute and mildly weird.

item.title: Let's Hope This Lives
item.content:encoded:
Alright, this is my third skip. Hopefully. Hopefully it works. What was I going for here? Well, originally I had it as an army of woodboring beetles that were destroying a forest god, but that didn't work. Then I focused on making the bugs cuter while having the forest god in the background. In the end, I found out I just liked the bugs, so I removed the forest god altogether. And then I thought, but what do they do? After I figured out their "gimmick", I realised I didn't have to have them be sapient anymore, so I've left it debatable. I hope I have a good layer of mystery here.

EDIT: Made some last minute revisions, and there is now an addendum that I am proud of. Tell me what y'all think.
EDIT 2: Yay for +40! Is this a big landmark? No! But it's been at +39 for what has felt like forever! So I am glad to not stare at that 9. Taunting me. Thank you, for +40.
EDIT 3: Actually justified yay for +50! Yayy!

Years later, I made some art~!
Yayy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember critiquing this a while ago, but it seems cut down now. Or do I just have a terrible memory?
